articles.title: Nuro is testing its autonomous vehicle tech on Tokyo’s streets
articles.description: Nuro began testing its self-driving software on public roads in Tokyo, marking the first international expansion for the AV startup.

articles.description: The updates indicate that Google is focused on transforming Google Play into an enhanced gaming hub.

articles.title: Ford’s new AI assistant will help fleet owners know if seatbelts are being used
articles.description: Ford Pro AI debuted at Work Truck Week in Indianapolis and is now available to all of its U.S.-based Pro telematics subscribers.

articles.title: Ride-hailing inDrive acquires Pakistan’s Krave Mart to bolster grocery delivery
articles.description: inDrive has closed its acquisition of KraveMart, expanding grocery delivery in a market dominated by Foodpanda.

articles.title: Zendesk acquires agentic customer service startup Forethought

articles.title: India neobank Fi winds down banking services on its platform
articles.description: Fi, founded by former Google Pay executives, is discontinuing banking services over four years after launch.

articles.title: Netflix may have paid $600 million for Ben Affleck’s AI startup
articles.description: This deal could rank as among the streaming giant's largest acquisitions ever.

articles.title: Lovable says it added $100M in revenue last month alone, with just 146 employees
articles.description: Swedish vibe-coding unicorn Lovable crossed $400 million in annual recurring revenue (ARR) in February.

articles.title: Quince hits $10B valuation with giant $500M round led by Iconiq
articles.description: E-commerce company Quince has raised another massive round, less than a year after its previous raise.

articles.title: Alternative app store AltStore PAL joins the fediverse
articles.description: AltStore PAL now supports the fediverse, using its own Mastodon server to let iOS developers share app updates and alerts across platforms like Mastodon and Threads.

articles.title: Drivers in fatal Ford BlueCruise crashes were likely distracted before impact
articles.description: The National Transportation Safety Board released documents ahead of a March 31 hearing that help show how and why two drivers crashed into stationary vehicles in 2024, leading to three deaths.

articles.title: Replit snags $9B valuation 6 months after hitting $3B
articles.description: Replit raised a new $400 million round and said it hopes to have $1B in ARR by year's end.

articles.title: Pro-Iran hacktivist group says it is behind attack on medical tech giant Stryker
articles.description: The hacktivist group claimed the attack was in retaliation for a U.S. strike on a Tehran school that killed more than 175 people, most of them children.

articles.title: Breakout Ventures raises $114M fund to back AI science startups
articles.description: Breakout Ventures is finding growing success by backing early-stage startups in scientific fields such as biology and chemistry.

articles.description: With AI igniting an investor frenzy, more startups are achieving unicorn status every month.

articles.title: VC mega-funds are back with General Catalyst, Spark rumored to be raising billions
articles.description: General Catalyst is said to be raising $10 billion and Spark Capital $3 billion. This after several other firms have also raised mega-funds.

articles.description: For years, venture capitalists have been skeptical of beverage startups, citing thin margins and brutal distribution as reasons most brands never break out. digest:
## 🔬 Major Technical Developments

### BitNet: Inference framework for 1-bit LLMs
URL: https://github.com/microsoft/BitNet
Microsoft open-sources infrastructure for running extremely quantized language models
1-bit quantization represents the theoretical floor for model compression, potentially enabling LLM inference on edge devices without GPUs. Microsoft's move to open-source this signals they're betting on commoditized inference while competing on other layers. Watch for this to accelerate on-device AI deployment timelines by 12-18 months.

### Temporal: The 9-year journey to fix time in JavaScript
URL: https://bloomberg.github.io/js-blog/post/temporal/
JavaScript finally gets a proper date/time API after nearly a decade of standards work
Bloomberg's engineering team led this TC39 proposal to replace the notoriously broken Date object. The long timeline illustrates the coordination cost of web standards, but success here removes a persistent source of bugs in financial applications where timestamp precision matters. Enterprise fintech tooling gets marginally less painful.

### Making WebAssembly a first-class language on the Web
URL: https://hacks.mozilla.org/2026/02/making-webassembly-a-first-class-language-on-the-web/
Mozilla pushes WebAssembly beyond its current plugin-style role toward native browser citizenship
This architectural shift could unlock performance-critical applications currently trapped in native apps—CAD, video editing, scientific computing. Browser vendors aligning on this creates optionality for startups building compute-heavy web products without Electron's overhead.

## 💼 Business & Market Signals

### Google closes deal to acquire Wiz
URL: https://www.wiz.io/blog/google-closes-deal-to-acquire-wiz
Google completes its largest cybersecurity acquisition to date
After Wiz rejected a reported $23B offer in 2024, deal terms suggest Google paid a premium to close. This consolidates cloud security under hyperscaler control and pressures standalone security vendors like CrowdStrike and Palo Alto. GCP's security story just became significantly more credible for enterprise procurement.

### Lovable says it added $100M in revenue last month alone, with just 146 employees
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/11/lovable-says-it-added-100m-in-revenue-last-month-alone-with-just-146-employees/
Swedish vibe-coding startup hits $400M ARR with extraordinary revenue-per-employee metrics
$685K revenue per employee per month is unprecedented in software. This validates the AI-assisted development category while raising questions about sustainability—are these one-time purchases or recurring seats? If repeatable, traditional dev tooling companies face serious multiple compression.

### Replit snags $9B valuation 6 months after hitting $3B
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/11/replit-snags-9b-valuation-6-months-after-hitting-3b/
Replit triples valuation in six months, targets $1B ARR by year-end
A 3x valuation jump signals either genuine hypergrowth or peak froth in AI developer tools. The $1B ARR target would make Replit one of the fastest-scaling software companies ever. Combined with Lovable's numbers, suggests a generational shift in how software gets built—or a bubble forming in real-time.

### VC mega-funds are back with General Catalyst, Spark rumored to be raising billions
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/11/vc-mega-funds-are-back-with-general-catalyst-spark-rumored-to-be-raising-billions/
GC targeting $10B, Spark $3B as mega-fund activity resumes
LP appetite returning to venture signals risk-on sentiment in private markets. Mega-funds historically correlate with inflated valuations and longer hold periods. For special situations: expect more structured deals and recap opportunities as these funds deploy into a narrower set of AI winners.

## 📋 Case Studies & Deep Dives

### Many SWE-bench-Passing PRs would not be merged
URL: https://metr.org/notes/2026-03-10-many-swe-bench-passing-prs-would-not-be-merged-into-main/
METR finds AI coding benchmarks overstate real-world capability
The gap between "passes tests" and "production-ready code" turns out to be substantial. This has direct implications for anyone underwriting AI coding tool valuations—the productivity gains are real but smaller than headline benchmark numbers suggest. Tempers expectations for near-term developer replacement narratives.

### Your Duolingo Is Talking to ByteDance: Cracking the Pangle SDK's Encryption
URL: https://www.buchodi.com/your-duolingo-is-talking-to-bytedance-cracking-the-pangle-sdks-encryption/
Security researcher documents data flows from popular apps to ByteDance ad infrastructure
Consumer apps embedding Chinese ad SDKs creates regulatory exposure that most users and some operators don't fully appreciate. With CFIUS scrutiny intensifying, this creates diligence risk for any portfolio company using third-party ad monetization. Worth flagging in tech audits.

### I was interviewed by an AI bot for a job
URL: https://www.theverge.com/featured-video/892850/i-was-interviewed-by-an-ai-bot-for-a-job
First-person account of AI-conducted job screening reveals current state of HR automation
The candidate experience reportedly ranges from uncanny to frustrating. Enterprises are clearly deploying these tools despite rough edges, suggesting cost pressure is overriding candidate experience concerns. HireVue, Paradox, and similar vendors are gaining share even with imperfect products.

## ⚡ Signal vs. Noise

The AI developer tools market is exhibiting classic bubble dynamics—Lovable and Replit posting numbers that would be extraordinary in any era, while METR's research suggests the underlying capabilities are more modest than benchmarks imply. The smart money is flowing to infrastructure (Microsoft's BitNet, Google's Wiz acquisition) rather than chasing application-layer valuations. Most telling signal: mega-funds returning to venture just as the AI hype cycle approaches what feels like peak narrative. Position for the inevitable correction in AI tooling multiples while infrastructure plays consolidate.
